title: nigritella rubra subsp. archiducis-joannis
intro: Nigritella rubra subsp. archiducis-joannis, also known as Archduke John's nigritella, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to the Alps. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of alkaloids, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Archduke John's nigritella include its potential to aid in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as its possible applications in treating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by extracting the alkaloids through a solvent, such as ethanol or water, and can be consumed in the form of teas, tinctures, or capsules.
